numpy_array:
import numpy as np
arr = np.array({data})
print(f'Numpy array:\n{arr}')
numpy_linspace:
import numpy as np
x = np.linspace({start}, {stop}, {num})
print(f'Linspace: {x}')
numpy_random:
import numpy as np
data = np.random.rand({shape})
print(f'Random data:\n{data}')
numpy_stats:
import numpy as np
data = np.array({data})
print(f'Mean: {np.mean(data)}, Std: {np.std(data)}')
matplotlib_bar:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.bar({categories}, {values})
plt.title('{title}')
plt.xlabel('{xlabel}')
plt.ylabel('{ylabel}')
plt.savefig('{filename}')
print(f'Saved bar chart to {filename}')
matplotlib_line:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot({x}, {y})
plt.title('{title}')
plt.savefig('{filename}')
print(f'Saved line chart to {filename}')
matplotlib_scatter:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.scatter({x}, {y})
plt.title('{title}')
plt.savefig('{filename}')
print(f'Saved scatter plot to {filename}')
seaborn_heatmap:
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({data})
sns.heatmap(df.corr(), annot=True)
plt.title('{title}')
plt.savefig('{filename}')
print(f'Saved heatmap to {filename}')
sklearn_split:
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split({X}, {y}, test_size={test_size})
print(f'Split data into {len(X_train)} train and {len(X_test)} test samples')
sklearn_linear_regression:
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
model = LinearRegression()
model.fit({X_train}, {y_train})
predictions = model.predict({X_test})
print(f'Linear Regression trained. R2 score: {model.score({X_test}, {y_test})}')
sklearn_random_forest:
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
model = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators={n_estimators})
model.fit({X_train}, {y_train})
print(f'Random Forest trained. Accuracy: {model.score({X_test}, {y_test})}')
sklearn_metrics:
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report, confusion_matrix
print(classification_report({y_true}, {y_pred}))
print(confusion_matrix({y_true}, {y_pred}))
pandas_iterrows:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({'name': ['Alice', 'Bob', 'Carol'], 'age': [30, 25, 35], 'city': ['Berlin', 'Munich', 'Hamburg']})

# Iterate over rows
for index, row in df.iterrows():
    print(f'Row {index}: {row["name"]} is {row["age"]} from {row["city"]}')

# Alternative: itertuples (faster)
for row in df.itertuples():
    print(f'{row.name} - {row.age}')
pandas_select_rows:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({'name': ['Alice', 'Bob', 'Carol', 'Dave'], 'age': [30, 25, 35, 28], 'score': [85, 92, 78, 95]})

# Select rows where age > 27
filtered = df[df['age'] > 27]
print('Age > 27:')
print(filtered)

# Multiple conditions
result = df[(df['age'] > 25) & (df['score'] > 80)]
print('\nAge > 25 and Score > 80:')
print(result)

# Using .loc
row = df.loc[df['name'] == 'Bob']
print(f'\nBob: {row.to_dict("records")}')
pandas_rename_cols:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({'old_name': ['Alice', 'Bob'], 'old_age': [30, 25]})
print('Before:', list(df.columns))

# Rename columns
df = df.rename(columns={'old_name': 'name', 'old_age': 'age'})
print('After:', list(df.columns))
print(df)
pandas_drop_column:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({'name': ['Alice', 'Bob'], 'age': [30, 25], 'temp': ['x', 'y']})
print('Before:', list(df.columns))

# Drop a column
df = df.drop(columns=['temp'])
print('After:', list(df.columns))
print(df)
pandas_row_count:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({'name': ['Alice', 'Bob', 'Carol'], 'age': [30, 25, 35]})

# Method 1: len()
print(f'Row count (len): {len(df)}')

# Method 2: shape
print(f'Shape: {df.shape} → {df.shape[0]} rows, {df.shape[1]} columns')

# Method 3: count per column
print(f'Count per column:\n{df.count()}')
